figures.caption: Table 1. Dynamic regret comparison of algorithms in PS episodic, finite-horizon MDPs and linear MDPs, under Assumptions 4.10 and 4.7. NT is the number of changes, S is the number of states, A is the number of actions, d is the dimension of the feature space for the linear case, T is the number of episodes and H is the number of steps per episode. Gray cells denote results of this work.
Figure 4. The transition kernel of MDPMi with ik = 1
Figure 1. Cumulative reward results for the experiments (higher=better). Top row: Tabular MDPs, Bottom row: Linear MDPs.

Figure 5. The process of transition probability assignment for the MDPs with NT = 2. The lines represent the value of the transition probability P k h,i(sg|leafℓ, a), and the intervals are the stationary segments. The colored triple underneath each interval denotes the triple (leaf ℓ̃i , ãi, h̃i) in (8).
